long_description:
Network redundancy loss during power maintenance event.
https://internal.softlayer.com/EventManagement/editNotification/171066008

fcs76a.sr02.ams03
fcs77a.sr02.ams03
fcs75a.sr02.ams03
fcs74a.sr02.ams03
fcs67a.sr02.ams03
fcs68a.sr02.ams03
bcs77a.sr02.ams03
bcs76a.sr02.ams03
bcs74a.sr02.ams03
bcs75a.sr02.ams03
bcs68a.sr02.ams03
bcs67a.sr02.ams03

Devices went down because the Powerfeed on the local switch powerstrip was the same as the powerfeed on the Aggregate powerstrip.
Wrong power redundancy connection.
short_description: Local Power Component - Cabling or Configuration Error - AMS03
close_notes: PWR - Cabling Issue - Cable(s) incorrect or not secured
current_status_next_steps:
Correct the power redundancy, Which has been done already.
All switches are now redundant and back online.
customer_facing_impact:
On 3 December 2025 at 06:15 UTC, the frontend customer switch (FCS), fcs75a.sr02.ams03 and backend customer switch (BCS), bcs75a.sr02.ams03 spontaneously rebooted due to a scheduled power maintenance . 
The switch recovered automatically and began forwarding customer traffic normally by 3 December 2025 06:30 UTC.

During this 15 minute period, customer servers located in rack 75, server room 02, data center ams03 would have experienced a disruption in frontend network access and backend network access for the duration of this incident.

Network specialists will continue to monitor the device to determine whether additional action may be necessary to prevent a recurrence of this issue. We apologize for any issues which this incident may have caused.

EMS : https://internal.softlayer.com/EventManagement/editNotification/171201108

https://github.ibm.com/IaaS-NRE/nreincidents/issues/19733
